title: ptychotis verticillata
intro: Ptychotis verticillata, also known as sea whortleberry, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to coastal areas of Scotland and Ireland. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and combat infections. The health benefits of Ptychotis verticillata include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Ptychotis verticillata are typically made from the leaves and stems, which can be dried, powdered, or infused in hot water to create teas or infusions.
